ops.text:
E23 VERIFY LANE (independent recompute, no LLM spend). Re-derived every headline number with my own code straight from data/judged/*.json per-site verdict strings (not trusting stored correct/precision scalars).

RESULT: CONFIRMED. All 14 per-rule precisions reproduce exactly: R01=0.0 R02=0.0 R03=0.0 R04=0.125 R05=0.0 R06=0.125 R07=0.125 R08=0.125 R09=0.125 R10=1.0 R11=0.25 R12=0.875 R13=0.875 R14=0.125. n_rules=14, n_passing=3 (R10/R12/R13 at >=0.80), criterion_met=FALSE — matches out/rules.json top-level (n_rules=14,n_passing=3,criterion_met=false,threshold=0.8).

CHECKS: (1) all 14 bootstrap CIs reproduce exactly under finalize.py's RNG (seed=23, B=5000, rules.json order); (2) internal consistency = 0 mismatches — each judged file's raw_response JSON verdicts equal its persisted site verdicts, and per-file scalars (n/correct/false_alarm/unclear) equal my recount; (3) judged sites trace to data/fired/*.json 'sample' in identical order (no site-swapping) for R10/R12/R13/R14/R01; (4) pre-registered criterion '>=10 rules at >=80% judged precision' evaluated exactly as registered — no goalpost move, no cherry-picking (all 14 rules incl. failures counted), and the result went AGAINST the hypothesis (3<10). R14 gap-fill (correct=1/fa=7 -> 0.125) confirmed via its raw_response. Not a PENDING-E15 promote/kill verdict — E23's criterion is its own intrinsic pre-registered hypothesis test. Verify artifacts: /Users/yasyf/.cc-steer/experiments/E23-verify/recompute.py.
